setting.note: A young nobleman crosses Napoleonic Europe and the intrigues of a tiny Italian court. The map runs across northern Italy: Milan under the French, the family castle on Lake Como, and the little despotic state of Parma with its citadel prison, where the story ends in a monastery. One scene falls far outside the setting, at Waterloo in Belgium, where the hero wanders the battle without ever grasping it.

rights.originalWork: The original French is public domain (Stendhal died 1842). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.attribution: The Charterhouse of Parma, Stendhal (1839). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or estate.

palette.note: The blue-grey of Lake Como and the parapets of the Farnese Tower, the two waters and heights the story turns on. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
